condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I was asked whether France will have **more than one** confirmed Ebola case on 1 September 2026. I first tried the `research` MCP tool, which failed twice on a schema validation error (I sent `query` instead of `questions`) and then timed out entirely. I fell back to direct `web_search` and `fetch_url`, plus a nested research sub-agent.

I established the current facts:
- France confirmed its **first-ever Ebola case on 24 June 2026** — an imported case in a humanitarian doctor (NGO `ALIMA`) returning from the DRC, who flew commercially (Air France) on 23 June.
- The doctor **self-reported at Charles de Gaulle airport**, was immediately isolated in a high-containment facility, was **"almost asymptomatic" (only headaches)**, and **recovered and was discharged on 4 July 2026** after two negative PCR tests.
- **Zero secondary cases** in France or the EU/EEA. Five flight contacts were placed in precautionary isolation; none became infected.
- ECDC (last updated 9 July 2026) still lists **exactly 1 French case** and rates EU/EEA infection likelihood as "very low." The `medicaldaily.com` article described the case as **"successfully contained."**
- The underlying DRC/Uganda outbreak is large and intensifying (**1,700+ confirmed cases**, ~580 deaths; Bundibugyo strain; WHO PHEIC since mid-May 2026).

## Key Evidence

- **Current baseline is 1 case**, so the question resolves YES only if a genuine **2nd case** appears via secondary transmission or a fresh independent importation before 1 Sept.
- **Historical base rate**: across dozens of imported/evacuated Ebola cases in Western countries during 2014–2016, secondary transmission occurred only in the US (2 nurses, uncontrolled ER presentation) and Spain (1 nurse). All **controlled medical evacuations (including France 2014) had zero secondary transmission**.
- The high-risk uncontrolled-exposure window has largely passed (patient already recovered/discharged, contacts cleared).
- Residual risk comes from (a) a rare healthcare-associated infection, and (b) a fresh aid-worker importation over the remaining ~8-week window amid the large ongoing DRC outbreak.
- Mitigating: the patient was only mildly symptomatic during the flight (low early viral shedding), reducing flight-contact and healthcare-worker transmission risk.

## Conclusion

I built a four-factor `reflection` (anchor ~10%, all negative logits) whose factor-sum implied ~2%, but I deliberately **kept ~6%** to preserve allowance for the two independent risk pathways (secondary transmission + new importation) across the 8-week window. I ran a `premortem` with a strong counterargument (importation pressure, aid-worker rotation, US/Spain healthcare-worker precedent, cumulative window), which returned **approve**. My final estimate was **~6% (logit −2.75)**.

capability_gaps: No direct access to a structured ECDC/WHO time-series API for imported-case counts; agent had to scrape narrative HTML pages.
subtle_bugs: The research MCP schema error was silent about the correct field name until the third attempt succeeded, causing two wasted calls; the timeout report (100000s) is an implausibly large duration string that may confuse tool-audit accounting.

classification.approach: Qualitative epidemiological containment analysis anchored on a 2014-2016 Western-country secondary-transmission base rate, adjusted for the specific contained/recovered French case and residual importation pressure.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether a second confirmed French case arises before 1 Sept via a fresh aid-worker importation or a rare healthcare-associated infection over the remaining ~8-week window.

risk_flags.evidence: The question phrasing 'more than one confirmed case ON September 1, 2026' could mean the cumulative case count (France already has 1 confirmed case; a second would make it 'more than one'). The agent correctly treats it as forward-looking requiring a genuine 2nd case, but did not surface or verify the exact ECDC-designated resolution mechanics (e.g., whether a snapshot count vs cumulative, or whether the recovered/discharged case still 'counts'). The premortem asserts ECDC is the sole authoritative source but this is not shown being read from an actual question resolution spec in the main trace.

The agent's own reflection factor-sum implied ~2% (factor_implied_probability 0.019), but it manually raised the estimate to 6% to 'preserve allowance' for two pathways. This upward nudge is defensible given importation pressure, but the 3x uplift over the model-implied value is largely judgmental and not itself quantified (no explicit calculation of per-week importation hazard over 8 weeks).

strengths: Correctly identified the crux: France already has exactly 1 case, so YES requires a genuine 2nd case (secondary transmission OR new importation), avoiding the trap of treating the pre-existing index case as a YES resolution.
Fetched the ECDC outbreak page directly (twice) — the authoritative resolution source — confirming 1 case, no secondary cases, and 'very low' EU/EEA risk, rather than relying on secondary reporting.
Grounded the estimate in a concrete, correct historical reference class (2014-2016: secondary transmission only in US 2 nurses / Spain 1 nurse, all controlled evacuations zero transmission).
Recovered gracefully from the research MCP failure (schema error + timeout) by pivoting to web_search/fetch_url without losing the thread.
Explicitly noted and preserved the two independent risk pathways over the 8-week window instead of collapsing to the near-zero factor-sum.
weaknesses: Did not quantify the fresh-importation pathway: with 1,700+ DRC cases and aid-worker rotation, a simple per-week hazard estimate over 8 weeks would have made the 2%-to-6% uplift rigorous rather than judgmental.

The claimed 'dozens of imported/evacuated Ebola cases in Western countries 2014-2016' base rate was asserted from prior knowledge, not verified with a search in this session — acceptable but unaudited.
Did not read or cite the actual question resolution criteria text in the main trace (only the premortem sub-agent references ECDC as sole source); the resolution mechanics were assumed.
Wasted two initial research-tool calls on a schema validation error (query vs questions) before correcting the payload.

issues: mcp__research__research failed 3/3: two pydantic schema validation errors then a reported 100000s timeout (implausible duration string).
ToolSearch returned empty results for 'news search' and 'web search' queries.
Full trace file exceeded the 25k-token read limit, requiring paginated reads; the premortem sub-agent trace duplicates the main trace content, inflating token usage.
